The Meteorite Story

Before Earth existed,
your ring was already forming.

Gibeon meteorite cooled at approximately 1°C per million years inside an ancient asteroid — so slowly that vast interlocking crystals of iron and nickel grew into geometric perfection. These are the Widmanstätten figures: cosmic fingerprints that no forge on Earth can recreate. Namibia's government now protects Gibeon as a national monument. The supply is finite. Your piece is singular.

Read the Full Story →

The Widmanstätten Pattern

Cooling at 1°C per million years, iron-nickel crystals grew into interlocking geometric formations unlike anything produced on Earth. Etched with acid, these ancient figures emerge like stars drawn in metal.

Learn the science →

4.5 Billion Years Old

Before Earth formed, the parent asteroid of Gibeon meteorite drifted through space. It fell near Gibeon, Namibia approximately 30,000 years ago — long before the first human civilization. It has been waiting for you.

Our full origin story →

Utterly Singular

Every meteorite inlay is cut from a unique fragment. Your ring's Widmanstätten pattern has never appeared on any other piece and never will. No duplicates. No mass production. One cosmos, one ring.

Start designing yours →

Protected & Finite

Since 2004, Namibia has declared Gibeon meteorite a protected national monument. No new collection is permitted. The supply in circulation is all that exists. When it's gone, it's gone — forever.

Why this matters →

Handmade in Minneapolis

Johan shapes, cuts, etches, inlays, and polishes every single piece by hand in the USA. No factory. No offshore shortcuts. Each ring carries hours of skilled artisan work alongside billions of years of cosmic formation.

Meet the maker →

The Collection

Every Piece, One of a Kind

Browse the full range of meteorite jewelry — from bold men's bands to sculptural engagement rings.
